description: Core prescriptive Part 9 rules for Housing and Small Buildings. RASE-inspired: Applicability, Requirement, Exception fields. All numeric values MUST be verified against the published NBC 2020 text before any demonstration (see prompt_plan.md task V1). Each rule carries a verification_notes object recording the evidence: quote (verbatim provision text), sources (URLs or document citations), verified_date (ISO date), reviewer, and free-text notes. verified_against_code_text may only be true when quote and sources are populated.

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except as provided in Article 9.8.4.7. and except for stairs serving areas only used as service rooms or service spaces, the rise, which is measured as the vertical nosing-to-nosing distance, shall comply with Table 9.8.4.1. [Table 9.8.4.1.: Private — Max. 200 mm, Min. 125 mm]
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-22; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 125-200 mm CONFIRMED for private stairs; limits live in Table 9.8.4.1, cite updated. Rise measured as vertical nosing-to-nosing distance. 'Private' = stairs serving single dwelling units, houses with secondary suite incl. common spaces, or garages serving those. Exceptions not encoded: service room/space stairs, spiral stairs (9.8.4.7). Values unchanged from NBC 2015.

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except for stairs serving areas only used as service rooms or service spaces, the run shall comply with Table 9.8.4.2. [Table 9.8.4.2.: Private — Max. 355 mm, Min. 255 mm] 2) The depth of a rectangular tread shall be not less than its run and not more than its run plus 25 mm.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 pp. 9-22/9-23; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 255-355 mm run CONFIRMED for private stairs (limits in Table 9.8.4.2). IMPORTANT: run and tread DEPTH are distinct — Sentence (2) requires run <= depth <= run+25 mm, so depth may legitimately reach 380 mm. Do NOT validate tread depth against 255-355. Fact-to-fact comparison (depth vs run) not expressible in engine v1 — future engine feature. 255 mm minimum dates from NBC 2015 (not a 2020 change).

rules.title: Stair width — stairs within dwelling units

verification_notes.quote: 4) Except as provided in Article 9.8.4.7., at least one stair between each floor level within a dwelling unit, and exterior stairs serving a single dwelling unit except required exit stairs, shall have a width of not less than 860 mm.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-21; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 860 mm CONFIRMED for in-unit stairs — Sentence (4), cite corrected from article level. Sentence map: (1) required exit/public stairs, residential: >=900 mm; (2) exit stairs serving a single dwelling unit or secondary-suite house: >=860 mm; (4) in-unit + exterior single-dwelling stairs: >=860 mm. KNOWN OVER-STRICTNESS: Sentence (4) requires only AT LEAST ONE stair between each floor level to meet 860 mm; engine v1 checks every private flight. A 900 mm shared-stair rule is a future addition (needs service='shared' facts).

verification_notes.quote: 3) Except as provided in Article 9.8.4.7., the clear height over stairs serving a single dwelling unit or a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces shall not be less than 1 950 mm.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-21; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 1950 mm CONFIRMED for stairs serving a single dwelling unit — Sentence (3), cite corrected from article level. Sentence (1): measured vertically over the clear width, from line tangent to nosings to lowest point above. Sentence (2): general stairs >=2050 mm (future shared-stair rule). Sentence (4), NEW in NBC 2020: >=1850 mm under beams/ducting in secondary suites.

verification_notes.quote: 2) Except as provided in Sentence (3) and Clause 3.8.3.5.(1)(e), required handrails shall be 865 mm to 1 070 mm high. [Sentence (1): measured vertically from top of handrail to a line tangent to tread nosings, or to the ramp/floor/landing surface. Sentence (3): handrails installed in addition to required handrails need not comply.]
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-28; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
Cross-checked: VBBL/bcpublications and code-consulting sources agree
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 865-1070 mm CONFIRMED, applies to REQUIRED handrails; v1 assumes every modeled handrail is required (supplementary handrails exempt per Sentence (3) — needs is_required fact to encode). History flag: pre-2015 max was 965 mm; Ontario only moved to 1070 in 2022 — do not trust older secondary sources.

rules.title: Guard height — guards within dwelling units

verification_notes.quote: 2) All guards within dwelling units or within houses with a secondary suite including their common spaces shall be not less than 900 mm high.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-30; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: RE-ENCODED during V1: original two rules used a pure fall-height trigger (900 if <=1800, 1070 if >1800), which is NOT the article's structure. Actual structure: default 1070 (S1); interior in-unit guards 900 regardless of fall height (S2); exterior single-dwelling guards 900 only where walking surface <=1800 mm above finished ground (S3). This rule encodes S2. Requires new guard_context fact: interior_dwelling_unit | exterior_single_dwelling | other.

rules.title: Guard height — exterior guard serving one dwelling unit, walking surface not more than 1800 mm above finished ground

verification_notes.quote: 3) Exterior guards serving not more than one dwelling unit or a house with a secondary suite including their common spaces shall be not less than 900 mm high where the walking surface served by the guard is not more than 1 800 mm above the finished ground level.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-30; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: Encodes S3. fall_height_mm here means height of walking surface above finished ground level. See interior-unit rule notes for the re-encoding rationale.

rules.title: Guard height — exterior guard serving one dwelling unit, walking surface more than 1800 mm above finished ground

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except as provided in Sentences (2) and (3), all guards shall be not less than 1 070 mm high.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-30; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: S1 default applies because the S3 carve-out (exterior single-dwelling) is limited to walking surfaces <=1800 mm above finished ground. See interior-unit rule notes for the re-encoding rationale.

rules.title: Guard height — all other guards (common areas, guards serving more than one dwelling unit)

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except as provided in Sentences (2) and (3), all guards shall be not less than 1 070 mm high.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 p. 9-30; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: S1 default for guards matching neither carve-out. Sentence (4) is a measurement rule (top of guard to line through tread nosings for flights), not a height value. Ontario OBC adds extra sentences (920 mm exit stairs, 1500 mm >10 m above grade) — Ontario-only, not national text.

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except as permitted in Sentences (3) and (4), openings through guards shall be of a size that prevents the passage of a spherical object having a diameter of 100 mm.
sources: NBC 2020 official PDF, publications.gc.ca NR24-28/2020E-PDF, https://publications.gc.ca/collections/collection_2022/cnrc-nrc/NR24-28-2020-eng.pdf (Div B Vol 2 pp. 9-30/9-31; verbatim in reference/nbc2020.txt)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 100 mm sphere rule CONFIRMED for required guards. Operator corrected <= to <: an opening that admits a 100 mm sphere must be smaller than 100 mm, so max_opening_mm == 100 would not 'prevent passage'. Exceptions not encoded (out of dwelling scope or need more facts): S2 150 mm sphere for stair-triangle openings; S3 industrial 535 mm allowance; S4 non-required guards may alternatively permit a 200 mm sphere. Anti-climb is separate Article 9.8.8.6 (>4.2 m protected height).

rules.title: Bedroom egress window — unobstructed open area

exceptions.description: Sentence (1) opening qualifier: 'Except where the suite is sprinklered' — the only true exception in the article

Sentence (1) alternative (not a formal exception): the requirement is for 'at least one outside window OR exterior door openable from the inside without the use of keys, tools or special knowledge' — a bedroom with a conforming exterior door needs no egress window

verification_notes.quote: 1) Except where the suite is sprinklered, each bedroom or combination bedroom shall have at least one outside window or exterior door openable from the inside without the use of keys, tools or special knowledge and without the removal of sashes or hardware. 2) The window referred to in Sentence (1) shall a) provide an unobstructed opening of not less than 0.35 m2 in area with no dimension less than 380 mm, and b) maintain the required opening during an emergency without the need for additional support.
sources: NRC Publications Archive, National Building Code of Canada 2020, https://nrc-publications.canada.ca/eng/view/object/?id=515340b5-f4e0-4798-be69-692e4ec423e8 (Division B, Vol 2; verbatim text confirmed in local extract reference/nbc2020.txt)
BCBC 2018 Div B 9.9, free.bcpublications.ca — word-for-word identical (values unchanged NBC 2015 -> 2020)
verification_notes.reviewer: Claude (research agent + direct check of official NRC PDF text)
verification_notes.notes: 0.35 m2 / 380 mm CONFIRMED, but they live in Sentence (2), not (1) — provision cite corrected. Exception structure corrected: sprinklered-suite qualifier is the only true exception; exterior door is an in-sentence alternative. Sentence (2)(b) 'without additional support' justifies not deriving open area from overall dims (operation type matters). NBC sets NO maximum sill height (appendix note recommends <=1.5 m, advisory only). Window wells need 760 mm clearance (Sentence (3)) — candidate future rule. WARNING: Ontario OBC differs materially (per-floor-level requirement, 1000 mm max sill height, 'without tools' only).

rules.title: Ceiling height — living and dining rooms

exceptions.description: Sloped-ceiling / partial-area provisions in 9.5.3 apply; v1 engine evaluates flat minimum only (verify full sentence structure)

verification_notes.notes: Values encoded from memory; awaiting verification against published NBC 2020 text (task V1).
